name: FETAL GASTROINTESTINAL ANOMALIES
chapter: DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS OF OBSTETRIC AND GYNECOLOGIC

demographics.Etiology: intestinal atresia / stenosis secondary to vascular accident, volvulus, meconium
demographics.Associated with: GI anomalies in 45% (malrotation, duplication, microcolon, esophageal
demographics.Incidence: abdominal cyst in 1÷500 - 1÷1,000 live births; 1÷2,625 pregnancies

dominant_finding: multiple distended bowel loops > 7 mm in diameter
discriminating_features: multiple distended bowel loops > 7 mm in diameter
polyhydramnios (if obstruction above level of mid jejunum; exceptions are esophageal

general: multiple distended bowel loops > 7 mm in diameter
polyhydramnios (if obstruction above level of mid jejunum; exceptions are esophageal

clinical.Management: parental testing for cystic fibrosis, careful fetal anatomic survey, follow-up
